text: Rename Installer agent to Auto-MergeInstall
Rename Tuner agent to Auto-Tune
Wire auto-tune into the observability and compound learning outcomes. Need to use auto-tune to apply the learning outcomes from observation and compound learning skills.
Change documentation structure to reduce size of ReadMe file. Offload details to other pages. Focus on high value highlights.

New skill template: shipment-reconcile — GI/GR double-entry integrity check for shipment manifests. Provides pre-mode (before backlogit_ship_shipment) and post-mode (after archive+restore) verification. Classifies manifest items as matched/pre-archived/missing/status-mismatch/orphan. Report-and-halt only, never auto-mutates. Proven in agent-engram. Gate on FEATURE_SHIPMENTS. Source: agent-engram/.github/skills/shipment-reconcile/SKILL.md

Ship agent template: add shipment close protocol — Step 0.5.6 (intake reconciliation check with shipment-reconcile mode:pre expected_status:queued) and Step 6.1 (full pre-archive gate, backlogit_ship_shipment call, archive integrity verify via P-007, post-archive reconciliation, commit). Includes file-lock integration. Gate on FEATURE_SHIPMENTS. Use templatized tool vars. Source: agent-engram ship.agent.md Steps 0.5 and 6.

Stage agent template: add scope guard at harvest (Step 5.5.3 sub-step 0) — mandatory first step that records harvest output IDs as harvest_ids and restricts add_to_shipment calls to only those IDs. Prevents over-inclusion of pre-existing queue items into shipments. Discovered after actual incident in agent-engram. Gate on FEATURE_SHIPMENTS. Source: agent-engram stage.agent.md Step 5.5.3.

New policy P-007: Backlogit Archive Integrity After Shipment — post-merge closure gate that detects and repairs the backlogit_ship_shipment file-deletion quirk (tool archives files then deletes archive copies from disk). Required action: git status, git restore .backlogit/archive/, stage, commit. Gate on FEATURE_SHIPMENTS. Add to workflow-policies.md.tmpl with amendment log entry. Source: agent-engram workflow-policies.md.

AGENTS.md template: add shipment-reconcile to Release Unit Pipeline — two conditional lines showing reconciliation at Stage Step 5.5 (scope guard) and Ship Step 6 (GI/GR gate: pre + post). Gate on FEATURE_SHIPMENTS. Source: agent-engram AGENTS.md pipeline section.

Harvest skill template: add 'no shipment creation' guardrail — explicit boundary that shipment creation is a downstream Ship concern, not a harvest responsibility. Separation-of-concerns guardrail. Gate on FEATURE_SHIPMENTS. Source: backlogit harvest/SKILL.md guardrails section.

Enforce strict Markdown heading hierarchy and markdownlint-cli pre-commit hook: ALL Markdown files must conform to a strict hierarchy of block-level elements starting with a single H1 header (no skipped levels, single H1 per file). A markdownlint-cli linter is required in all projects that use Markdown, enforced via a pre-commit hook that throws on violations. Scope: (1) Add markdownlint-cli as required tool in workspace discovery and harness installation, (2) Generate .markdownlint.json config enforcing heading hierarchy rules (MD001, MD025, MD041), (3) Generate pre-commit hook running markdownlint on staged .md files, (4) Update templates so all generated Markdown artifacts conform, (5) Add markdownlint verification to install-harness and tune-harness workflows.

Git merge policy: always use merge commits, expressly forbid squash merge. Squash merge must be forbidden by policy and enforced at multiple layers: (1) Workflow policy entry forbidding squash merge with violation action, (2) Constitution/foundation doc principle requiring merge commits for full commit history preservation, (3) GitHub repo settings template recommending disable of squash merge option, (4) Pre-merge guardrail in Ship agent to verify merge strategy before PR merge, (5) CI check or pre-commit hook to detect and reject squash merge attempts. Rationale: merge commits preserve the full development history, individual commit attribution, and bisect-friendly history.

fix-ci skill template v2 overhaul — major rewrite incorporating 10 generalizable improvements from backlogit: (1) extended input schema with poll-interval/max-wait, (2) dedicated Copilot review comment detection step, (3) hard reply gate (NON-NEGOTIABLE: every review thread must have reply before completion), (4) cascade restart when fixes regress earlier gates, (5) defect logging step for unresolved CI failures as backlog items, (6) prerequisites/quick-start/parameters sections, (7) explicit subagent execution constraint, (8) granular intercom event table (17 events), (9) terminal output management guidance, (10) common fix patterns taxonomy per check type. Source: backlogit fix-ci/SKILL.md.

Self-install support: teach auto-mergeinstall to handle the autoharness workspace — (1) relax the workspace_path != autoharness_home guard in install-harness Step 1.0 when target is autoharness itself, (2) add workspace profile signal (e.g. distribution.global_agents_dir) that tells installer to place workflow agents in .github/local-agents/ instead of .github/agents/ when the target distributes agents globally, (3) ensure pyproject.toml force-include only maps .github/agents/ (not local-agents/) so workflow agents never leak into the wheel, (4) configure .vscode/settings.json chat.agentFilesLocations to include both directories. Infrastructure already partially implemented: pyproject.toml updated, .vscode/settings.json updated, .github/local-agents/ created with placeholder stage/ship agents.

Teach tune-harness to respect distribution.local_agents_dir routing — when tuning an autoharness self-install, workflow agent drift checks should target .github/local-agents/ instead of .github/agents/. Follow-up from 004-F plan-review finding #2.

Add verify-workspace hardening for the Auto-Tune learning loop: assert that auto-tune.agent.md and tune-harness/SKILL.md retain the Step 1.8 contract to mine compound learnings, continuous-learning state, and closure artifacts into learning-driven tuning proposals.
Add a fixture-based test that proves Auto-Tune converts real compound entries into learning-driven tuning output, so the workspace-learning feedback loop is validated beyond prompt text alone.

There seems to be an issue with the initial install of autoharness that it is not properly enforcing the workflow boundaries of the Stage and Ship agents.  I have repeatedly found the Stage agent both ignoring this boundary and shipping the code also while ignoring the mandate to always first push code on a feature or chore branch and by submitting a PR.

Need to add a checklist at the start of each agent session to gate the workflow on a pre-flight check on the availability of all of the MCP tools or other tools indicated.  Too many times copilot starts grepping files because it thinks the tools are unavailable, which is unacceptable.

Need a way to force the Stage and Ship agents to each create a branch for their work and a strategy for allowing these agents to be run in parallel or asynchronously without stepping on each other.  Also need an overview dispatcher agent that effectively orchestrates both the Stage and Ship agents as invoked subagents for continuous iteration.
Need to add a template command and gate for each agent to run backlogit_sync_index prior to the start of each agent session and at the end of each agent session.
Need an end of closure gate that includes a step to resynch the backlogit db based on the recent changes.  This can be an MCP tool call by the agent.
Add a deterministic post-merge closure gate: do not begin the Ship post-merge closure process until the feature or chore branch has actually been merged and the ship PR is confirmed closed.

Evaluate karpathy-guidelines as a candidate instructions template. ATV's karpathy-guidelines skill codifies behavioral rules to reduce common LLM coding mistakes: Think Before Coding (surface assumptions), Simplicity First (no speculative features), Surgical Changes (touch only what you must), and Goal-Driven Execution (define success criteria, loop until verified). autoharness constitution already covers some of this but the karpathy framing is more concise and action-oriented. Could be adapted as templates/instructions/coding-discipline.instructions.md.tmpl with applyTo: '**' as a lightweight reinforcement layer. Deepens Primitive 6 (Injection Points). Source: atv-starterkit/pkg/scaffold/templates/skills/karpathy-guidelines/SKILL.md

Evaluate expanded review persona library for conditional activation. ATV now ships 52 review persona agents including specialized reviewers for: adversarial chaos engineering, API contract changes, data integrity, data migrations, performance (N+1 queries, unbounded allocations), reliability, schema drift detection, code simplicity, design-implementation alignment, and feasibility. autoharness has a review skill with conditional persona support but a smaller persona catalog. Worth evaluating which personas should become standard conditional templates vs. which are too domain-specific. High-value candidates: adversarial-reviewer, performance-reviewer, reliability-reviewer, data-migrations-reviewer, schema-drift-detector. Source: atv-starterkit/pkg/scaffold/templates/agents/ (52 files), atv-starterkit/.github/agents/ (52 files)

Evaluate SDK intelligence layer patterns for harness resilience. ATV's pkg/sdk/intelligence.go implements rate limiting (min interval between queries, hourly query budget), circuit breaker (3 consecutive failures triggers 5-minute cooldown), and graceful offline degradation. autoharness circuit-breaker instructions template already exists but these SDK-level patterns could inform more concrete circuit-breaker and concurrency guardrail templates with specific defaults and state tracking patterns. Deepens Primitive 5 (Guardrails). Source: atv-starterkit/pkg/sdk/intelligence.go, atv-starterkit/pkg/sdk/intelligence_test.go

Evaluate plugingen audit pattern for template marketplace compatibility. ATV's pkg/plugingen/audit.go implements a deterministic audit that scans skills and agents for marketplace-incompatible content (hardcoded plugin cache paths, namespaced agent references, MCP plugin tool names) using regex rules with sortable findings. autoharness verify_workspace.py already does template validation but doesn't have equivalent portability audits. Could inform a verify_workspace assertion that checks installed harness artifacts don't contain environment-specific references that would break cross-environment portability. Deepens environment agnosticism. Source: atv-starterkit/pkg/plugingen/audit.go, atv-starterkit/pkg/plugingen/generate.go

Investigate value of including a policy template for auto-tune to use in building additional policies for a workspace as new requirements are surfaced through compound learning and by the human operator.

graphtor-docs capability pack: Schema registration — Add "graphtor-docs" to capability_packs enum in harness-config.schema.json (line 41) and workspace-profile.schema.json (lines 166, 193). Add optional graphtor_docs config block to harness-config.schema.json (sibling of continuous_learning/strict_safety) with fields: sources_config_path (default .graphtor/config/sources.yaml), auto_sync (boolean), binary_path (string). Three enum sites + one config object.

graphtor-docs capability pack: Profile detection block — Add graphtor_docs detection object to workspace-profile.schema.json (after agent_engram block ~line 257) matching the existing pack detection pattern: detected (bool), mcp_configured (bool), config_paths (array), binary_available (bool), sources_configured (bool), tool_count (int, the 8 MCP tools), recommended (bool). Detection signals: .graphtor/ directory exists, .mcp.json contains graphtor-docs server entry, graphtor-docs binary on PATH or at .graphtor/bin/graphtor-docs, .graphtor/config/sources.yaml exists.

graphtor-docs capability pack: Instruction template — Create templates/instructions/graphtor-docs.instructions.md.tmpl teaching agents how to use the 8 MCP tools (search_local_docs, search_semantic, research_topic, traverse_doc_links, list_sources, get_chunk_by_id, get_document, get_status). Include: tool selection guide, typical workflow patterns (targeted lookup, topic exploration, conceptual search), source configuration via .graphtor/config/sources.yaml, sync requirement before first use. Follow the same structure as agent-engram.instructions.md.tmpl and agent-intercom.instructions.md.tmpl.

graphtor-docs capability pack: Install-harness SKILL.md updates — Add graphtor-docs pack installation steps in the overlay weaving section (~line 644-646). Steps: (1) install graphtor-docs.instructions.md from template, (2) weave graphtor-docs awareness into stage.agent.md.tmpl (learnings-researcher should search graphtor-docs before planning) and ship.agent.md.tmpl (build-feature should consult indexed docs for context), (3) add overlay_targets and verification_checks to manifest, (4) add template variables: GRAPHTOR_SOURCES_PATH (default .graphtor/config/sources.yaml), GRAPHTOR_BINARY_PATH (default graphtor-docs).

graphtor-docs capability pack: Tune-harness drift detection — Add graphtor-docs drift checks to tune-harness/SKILL.md (~line 301-303) following existing pattern: "graphtor-docs markers (.graphtor/ directory, MCP server config) detected but graphtor-docs pack not in capability_packs" triggers recommendation. Also add overlay coherence check at ~line 772-773 verifying graphtor-docs instruction file exists when pack is enabled and references the 8 tool names.

graphtor-docs capability pack: Agent template overlay weaving — Update templates/agents/stage.agent.md.tmpl and ship.agent.md.tmpl to include conditional graphtor-docs overlay sections (similar to agent-intercom/agent-engram overlays). Stage: learnings-researcher uses graphtor-docs search before deliberation/planning. Ship: build-feature consults indexed docs for implementation context. Both agents reference graphtor-docs instruction file when pack is enabled. Add overlay markers that tune-harness can detect.

graphtor-docs capability pack: Workspace discovery updates — Update workspace-discovery/SKILL.md to detect graphtor-docs presence during workspace scanning. Discovery should check: (1) .graphtor/ directory existence, (2) .mcp.json or .vscode/mcp.json for graphtor-docs server config, (3) graphtor-docs binary availability (PATH or .graphtor/bin/), (4) .graphtor/config/sources.yaml existence and validity, (5) populate graphtor_docs detection block in workspace-profile.yaml, (6) recommend graphtor-docs pack when detected=true and mcp_configured=true.

graphtor-docs capability pack: Verify-workspace enforcement — Add verification assertions to src/autoharness/verify_workspace.py for graphtor-docs pack: (1) when graphtor-docs is in capability_packs, graphtor-docs.instructions.md must exist in installed artifacts, (2) instruction file must reference all 8 MCP tool names, (3) overlay markers must be present in stage/ship agent files when pack is enabled. Add corresponding tests to tests/test_verify_workspace.py.

graphtor-docs capability pack: Harness architecture documentation — Update .github/instructions/harness-architecture.instructions.md to document graphtor-docs as a capability pack overlay. Add it to the overlay examples section alongside agent-intercom, browser-verification, continuous-learning, and strict-safety. Document which primitives it deepens: Primitive 1 (knowledge retrieval via indexed docs), Primitive 9 (repository knowledge augmented with external doc sources). Specify eligibility signals, overlay targets, behavior deltas, and verification checks per the Required Overlay Contract.

Community reference library via git submodules — Add the following agent/skill repositories as git submodules under a new references/ directory in the autoharness repo: (1) references/awesome-copilot — https://github.com/github/awesome-copilot, (2) references/awesome-agent-skills — https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-agent-skills, (3) references/awesome-claude-skills — https://github.com/ComposioHQ/awesome-claude-skills, (4) references/ai-skills — https://github.com/iliaal/ai-skills, (5) references/awesome-agents — https://github.com/kyrolabs/awesome-agents, (6) references/agent-skills — https://github.com/CommandCodeAI/agent-skills. These serve as read-only reference libraries for template authors and agents when curating templates/community/. Each submodule is pinned to a specific commit and periodically refreshed via git submodule update --remote. They are NOT deployed to target workspaces — they exist solely in the autoharness development workspace. Also: (a) configure .gitmodules with all 6 submodules, (b) document the reference library pattern in docs/ (purpose, how to refresh, how to use when authoring templates), (c) add a templates/community/ directory with a README explaining how curated templates are distilled from reference material, (d) update .gitignore if needed to exclude any build artifacts from submodule repos.

Community Reference Template Curation & Registry — Browse the 6 reference libraries in references/ (~3,992 files), identify high-value agent, skill, instruction, and prompt patterns, and distill them into {{VARIABLE}}-parameterized .tmpl files in templates/community/. Create a community template registry (e.g. templates/community/registry.yaml or a JSON schema) that catalogs every curated template with: template ID, artifact type (agent/skill/instruction/prompt), source repo + path, description of what it does, applicable technology profiles, prerequisite capability packs, and tags/categories. The registry enables autoharness install/tune workflows to select relevant community templates for a workspace without scanning all file content — the installer reads the registry, matches entries against the workspace profile, and proposes applicable templates. The registry schema should be added to schemas/ and referenced by install-harness and tune-harness skills. Curation criteria: technology-agnostic, valid Markdown after variable resolution, tested against 3+ profiles (Rust, Go, Python/TypeScript), source attribution in YAML frontmatter per templates/community/README.md conventions.

Orchestrator agent template lacks workspace identity verification — When installed into a target workspace, the orchestrator.agent.md.tmpl contains no {{PROJECT_NAME}} or workspace-identifying content. It references Stage and Ship generically but never anchors itself to the target workspace. This means: (1) An installed orchestrator cannot verify it's operating on the correct workspace (it could be invoked from the wrong directory), (2) There's no installed-time evidence that the orchestrator was customized for a specific workspace vs being a generic copy, (3) verify_workspace.py has no assertion to check that the orchestrator references the target workspace name.

Fix should include: (a) Add {{PROJECT_NAME}} to the orchestrator template so the installed agent says 'You are the Orchestrator for the {{PROJECT_NAME}} repository' (matching how stage.agent.md.tmpl and ship.agent.md.tmpl reference their workspace), (b) Add a verify_workspace assertion that checks the installed orchestrator.agent.md contains the resolved project name (not the template variable), (c) Check whether stage and ship agent templates also properly reference {{PROJECT_NAME}} and add assertions if missing. This is a quality/correctness issue — not a new feature.

Orchestrator Single-Command Pipeline Trigger — Add a simple operator command (e.g. 'run pipeline' or 'process stash') that triggers the Orchestrator to execute the full pipeline in one invocation: (1) Triage stash entries and group related items by theme/dependency, (2) Route grouped entries through Stage to produce shipments, (3) Hand off the first staged shipment to Ship for execution. Currently the operator must issue separate commands with manual handoff. The orchestrator template Step 0-3 pipeline exists but has no documented single-command entry point that chains triage-grouping-staging-shipping autonomously. Implementation: Add trigger phrases section to orchestrator.agent.md.tmpl, add grouping heuristic to Step 1 for thematic relatedness and dependency overlap. Touches: templates/agents/orchestrator.agent.md.tmpl.

Pre-Ship Staging Artifact PR Gate — Stage currently commits backlog artifacts (features, tasks, shipments) directly to main. Ship then creates a feature branch from main. But in repos with branch protection, Stage's direct commits to main may be blocked — and even without protection, staging artifacts should go through a PR for traceability. The fix: (1) Stage should commit backlog/planning artifacts to a staging branch (e.g. chore/stage-019-s), PR it to main, and merge before handing off to Ship. (2) The Orchestrator should enforce that the staging PR is merged before invoking Ship — Ship's Branch Creation Gate (P-011) already requires main to be clean and up-to-date, but there is no gate that checks whether the staging artifacts from Stage are actually on main yet. (3) Add a pre-Ship gate to the orchestrator template: after Stage completes, verify the staging artifacts are merged to main before routing to Ship. This ensures Ship always branches from a main that contains the shipment manifest it will claim. Touches: templates/agents/orchestrator.agent.md.tmpl (pre-Ship staging merge gate), templates/agents/stage.agent.md.tmpl (staging branch + PR workflow), templates/agents/ship.agent.md.tmpl (document the dependency). Needs deliberation on whether Stage should always PR or only when branch protection is detected.

Orchestrator Model Override in config.yaml — Add per-agent model configuration to the harness config schema so the orchestrator can be configured independently from its tier default. Currently, the orchestrator inherits from model_routing.tier2 (defaults to claude-sonnet-4.6). The operator wants to run the orchestrator on gpt-5.4 while keeping Stage on tier3 (opus-4.6) and Ship on tier2 (sonnet-4.6). Implementation: (a) Add model_routing.orchestrator block to harness-config.schema.json with model, reasoning_effort, model_provider, model_family fields (same shape as tier blocks), (b) Update install-harness variable resolution: add ORCHESTRATOR_MODEL, ORCHESTRATOR_PROVIDER, ORCHESTRATOR_FAMILY, ORCHESTRATOR_REASONING_EFFORT variables that resolve from config.model_routing.orchestrator first, falling back to tier2, (c) Update orchestrator.agent.md.tmpl frontmatter to use the new ORCHESTRATOR_* variables instead of TIER_2_* variables, (d) Default ORCHESTRATOR_FAMILY to gpt-5.4 when no override is set, (e) Update docs. Touches: schemas/harness-config.schema.json, install-harness SKILL.md (variable resolution table), templates/agents/orchestrator.agent.md.tmpl, docs.

Cross-Provider Model Routing Verification — Verify that the orchestrator agent can run on a different model provider (e.g. GPT-5.4 high from OpenAI) while successfully routing Stage to Opus 4.6 (Anthropic) and Ship to Sonnet 4.6 (Anthropic). Current template frontmatter declares model_tier, model_provider, and model_family for each agent, but there is no verification that: (1) the environment actually honors frontmatter model declarations when spawning subagents, (2) cross-provider routing works (orchestrator on GPT, subagents on Claude), (3) the model field in frontmatter is consumed by VS Code Copilot, Copilot CLI, Codex, Cursor, and Claude Code consistently. Verification tasks: (a) Document which environments support frontmatter model field and how each resolves it, (b) Test orchestrator on GPT-5.4 invoking Stage on Opus 4.6 in at least one environment, (c) Add a section to the orchestrator template documenting supported model routing combinations and known limitations, (d) If cross-provider routing is not natively supported in some environments, document the workaround (e.g. operator must manually switch models between Stage/Ship invocations). This is a verification/documentation chore, not a code change.

Automated PR Review Comment Resolution Loop — After Ship creates a PR, it currently waits for the operator to say "additional Copilot review comments to fix" and then manually triggers the fix cycle. The Ship agent should instead automatically: (1) After pushing and creating the PR, poll for Copilot review comments using gh api graphql (reviewThreads query), (2) If unresolved threads exist, read each comment, fix the issue, commit, push, reply to the comment, and resolve the thread programmatically, (3) Re-poll after fixes to check for new review comments triggered by the fix commit, (4) Repeat until zero unresolved threads remain OR the circuit breaker limit is hit (e.g. 3 fix-ci cycles per PR), (5) Only then present the PR as ready for operator merge approval. This loop should be templatized in ship.agent.md.tmpl as part of Step 4 (PR Lifecycle) so it works in any installed workspace. The fix-ci skill template should also be updated to handle Copilot review comments as a first-class CI signal alongside check suite failures. Key design decisions: poll interval between fix cycles, how to distinguish Copilot bot comments from human reviewer comments (author.login matching), whether to batch-fix all comments per cycle or fix one at a time, and the GraphQL queries needed (reviewThreads, addPullRequestReviewComment, resolveReviewThread).

Spike: Context Window Compaction Implementation Plan — Research and produce an implementation plan based on docs/design-docs/context-window-compaction-strategy.md. The design doc describes a three-layer pipeline strategy for keeping context windows lean in long-running agent sessions: (1) Tool Result Offloading — write tool responses over a threshold to disk, keep file path + preview in context, (2) Tool Input Eviction — delete raw inputs of old write commands after changes are committed, (3) Head Summarization + Tail Preservation — triggered at 85% capacity, summarize oldest messages via cheap LLM, preserve recent 10-15%, archive originals to disk. The spike should: (a) Evaluate which layers are implementable within the autoharness template/skill framework vs requiring environment-level support (e.g. Copilot CLI, Codex natively manage context), (b) Map each layer to autoharness primitives (Layer 1 maps to Primitive 1 state/context, Layer 3 maps to compact-context skill), (c) Assess what already exists — the compact-context skill template already handles some of Layer 3, (d) Identify gaps: Layer 1 and Layer 2 likely need environment hooks that autoharness templates cannot control, (e) Produce an implementation plan for what IS templateable: enhanced compact-context skill, new compaction-trigger instruction template, integration with agent-intercom for compaction broadcast events. Time-box: 2 hours.

Stage/Ship Handoff Hook System — Implement deterministic role enforcement for the two-agent workflow via the agent harness itself, not through individual capability packs or tools. Key constraint: backlogit and other tools can be used in isolation or together, so enforcement cannot live inside any single tool's server. The harness must be the enforcement point.

Design direction (harness-native enforcement):
(1) Agent definition templates declare their role identity and permitted operation classes (e.g. stage.agent.md.tmpl declares role: stage, permitted: [create_item, create_shipment, update_item, stash, search], forbidden: [claim_shipment, move_item_to_active, push, pr_create]).
(2) A new harness-level instruction template (e.g. role-enforcement.instructions.md.tmpl) defines the enforcement contract: before any mutation, the agent checks its declared role against the operation's classification. This is harness infrastructure, not pack-specific.
(3) The hook contract is templatized: install-harness weaves role declarations into agent templates based on which agents are installed and what workflow model is active (two-agent stage/ship vs single-agent). Workspaces without the two-agent model don't get role restrictions.
(4) Handoff events remain in backlogit hooks.yaml as an optional signal layer — but the primary enforcement is the harness instruction that gates the agent before it even calls the tool.
(5) Verification: verify_workspace.py checks that installed agent definitions declare consistent role permissions and that the role-enforcement instruction is present when two-agent workflow is active.

This keeps enforcement environment-agnostic (works in VS Code, Copilot CLI, Codex, Cursor, Claude Code) and tool-agnostic (works whether the workspace uses backlogit, backlog-md, or any other MCP tool).

Touches: templates/instructions/ (new role-enforcement template), templates/agents/ (role declarations in stage/ship), install-harness SKILL.md (conditional weaving), verify_workspace.py (role consistency checks), docs/. Needs deliberation to finalize the role declaration schema and enforcement instruction design.

Backlogit capability pack sync: 23 missing MCP tools + new features (058-F branch telemetry, 057-F schema introspection, 055-S lifecycle hygiene, 054 events harvest). Registry has 30/53 tools. Missing: stash mgmt (7), links (3), lifecycle (2), discovery (7), telemetry (1), deliberation (1), maintenance (2). Update: registry.yaml, backlogit instructions, SQL schema template, compatibility matrix.

Agent-engram capability pack sync: 8 missing MCP tools + new features. Engram has 20 tools total, autoharness documents 12 (9 core + set_workspace/sync_workspace/flush_state). Missing 8: query_graph_neighborhood (033-S new structured graph API), index_git_history (git blame/history indexing), get_workspace_statistics, get_health_report, get_evaluation_report, get_branch_metrics, get_token_savings_report, get_mutable_script_retry_metrics. New features: 034-S daemon startup reliability/OOM fix, 033-S query_graph structured API, 032-S CLI resilience/error handling, indexing resilience with SQLITE_BUSY guards. Update: agent-engram.instructions.md.tmpl to document observability/diagnostics tools and query_graph_neighborhood.

Orchestrator elective agent routing: Add Auto-MergeInstall and Auto-Tune as elective subagents invocable by the Orchestrator. Update the orchestrator.agent.md.tmpl to declare these as optional routing targets alongside Stage and Ship. The Orchestrator should be able to invoke Auto-MergeInstall (for workspace discovery + harness installation into a target workspace) and Auto-Tune (for iterative harness maintenance and drift detection) when the operator requests it. These are elective — not part of the default Stage/Ship pipeline — and should be documented as such in the agent definition. Update: orchestrator.agent.md.tmpl to add elective agent section, routing logic, and subagent depth rules for these agents.

Full merge-install of autoharness v1.4.0 into the backlogit workspace: Run Auto-MergeInstall against the backlogit repository (D:\Source\GitHub\backlogit) to bring its harness up to date with the current autoharness template structure. This includes workspace discovery, template composition with the backlogit workspace profile, and installation of all harness artifacts (agents, skills, instructions, policies, prompts, foundation docs). Use merge-install mode to preserve any existing customizations in the backlogit workspace. The autoharness home is D:\Source\GitHub\autoharness.

Full merge-install of autoharness v1.4.0 into the agent-engram workspace: Run Auto-MergeInstall against the agent-engram repository (D:\Source\GitHub\agent-engram) to bring its harness up to date with the current autoharness template structure. This includes workspace discovery, template composition with the agent-engram workspace profile, and installation of all harness artifacts (agents, skills, instructions, policies, prompts, foundation docs). Use merge-install mode to preserve any existing customizations. The autoharness home is D:\Source\GitHub\autoharness. NOTE: This PR may exceed the 20,000 line Copilot review limit. Run a full adversarial review of the git diff to identify problems before PR approval instead of relying solely on Copilot PR reviewer.

Housekeeping: commit pending backlog state and submodule updates to main. (1) Stage all .backlogit/ changes (archive files, logs, queue files for shipments 027-S through 029-S, stash.jsonl updates). (2) Update 3 reference submodules to latest: references/ai-skills, references/awesome-agent-skills, references/awesome-agents. (3) Commit and push to main via feature branch + PR. This should be grouped with the v1.4.0 version bump (stash E6BCB03C) into a single release shipment.

Orchestrator 'start assembly line' command — standard command that begins the orchestrator pipeline in a continuous loop (ralph loop), processing stash through Stage then Ship until empty or halted. Key requirement: Stage workflow must go through its own full branch + PR cycle, separate from Ship's branch + PR lifecycle. This ensures atomicity of action and clear ownership — Stage owns planning artifacts on its branch/PR, Ship owns implementation artifacts on its branch/PR. Neither agent's work is entangled in the other's commit history.

Subagent directory convention: All agent templates that are NOT top-level agents (Orchestrator, Stage, Ship, Auto-Tune, Auto-MergeInstall) should be stored in templates/agents/subagents/ rather than templates/agents/. This includes review personas, researchers, and any other leaf-executor or delegated agents. Update: (1) move existing non-top-level agent templates to templates/agents/subagents/, (2) update install-harness SKILL.md to install subagents to .github/agents/subagents/ in target workspaces, (3) update all cross-references in skills, instructions, and foundation docs that reference moved agents, (4) update verify_workspace.py if it checks agent paths, (5) update the dogfood self-install (.github/agents/) to match the new structure.

eval CLI: _eval_command help detection scans ALL remaining tokens, so 'eval review --base help' short-circuits to usage and a git ref literally named 'help' cannot be used as --base/--head. Fix by only treating a leading token (rest[0]) as a help request. Source: code-review P3 on 056-S, src/autoharness/cli.py _eval_command.

eval reviewer: parse_unified_diff misreads an added line whose content starts with '+++ ' as a new-file header (resets hunk state, drops remaining added/context lines). Fix by tracking the hunk header's declared new-line count (@@ +c,d @@) and stopping header detection once the hunk is exhausted. Source: code-review P3 on 056-S, src/autoharness/eval/reviewer.py:145-148.

Research how to get autoharness to engage in full dark factory mode safely while performing most code reviews locally and reducing reliance on GitHub Copilot reviews. Desired workflow: operator begins with substantial research for a new feature/epic, then uses a brainstorm skill to surface hard questions and decisions, then hands off to dark mode for fully autonomous implementation. Reference ATV Starter Kit for brainstorm skill design and implementation patterns.

Dark mode operating instructions: when running in dark mode, run the full Stage/Ship pipeline end-to-end until all stashed and/or queued work is completed or judged unsafe without operator input. Perform adversarial reviews of all code and decisions before submitting PRs. Permission is granted to open PRs and approve merges. Wait patiently for Copilot reviews, resolve all comments through as many iterations as needed, reply to each comment after fixes are committed and pushed, then resolve the thread programmatically with gh api graphql. Operator may be AFK/unavailable; use sound, well-reasoned autonomous judgment.
All modes operating rule: before submitting any PR where code has been added, removed, or changed, always run a full local build of the codebase to ensure it builds successfully.

Reduce CI build action usage so build jobs run only when code-relevant changes require them. Goal: avoid unnecessary GitHub Actions build minutes for docs/backlog/chore-only PRs while keeping required validation for code-impacting changes.

[COORDINATION from backlogit — gate-broker feature D23DFA0B] Add a structured `repeated_failure` object to the `autoharness gate check --json` correction report so the backlogit pre-task-completion gate broker can act deterministically on the repeated-failure outcome instead of parsing the human circuit-breaker checkpoint file.

BACKGROUND: backlogit will become the live enforcement point that calls `autoharness gate check` before writing a task/subtask -> done (and shipment -> shipped) transition. Backlogit owns durable backlog task state; autoharness owns the gate engine, the consecutive-failure counter (.autoharness/gates/gate-state.json), the on_repeated_failure policy (block|escalate), and max_gate_failures (default 3). A backlogit deliberation (backlogit repo docs/decisions/2026-07-05-gate-repeated-failure-requeue-ownership-deliberation.md) DECIDED that BACKLOGIT performs the requeue/escalate backlog transition, DRIVEN BY a signal autoharness surfaces — NOT autoharness shelling out to backlogit (that would break the gate subsystem isolation and create reentrancy).

WHAT TO BUILD IN AUTOHARNESS:
1. Extend the `gate check --json` correction report with a top-level `repeated_failure` object whenever gates ran, e.g.:
   { "repeated_failure": { "count": <int consecutive blocking failures for this task>, "threshold": <int max_gate_failures, default 3>, "reached": <bool count >= threshold>, "action": "block" | "escalate" (from on_repeated_failure) } }
   Emit it on BOTH blocked (exit 1) and pass (exit 0, with count reset to 0 and reached=false) so the caller can observe counter resets too.
2. Keep the existing exit-code contract UNCHANGED (0 pass/no-gates/advisory; 1 blocked; 2 invalid args/config). `repeated_failure` is additive metadata, not a new exit code.
3. Keep writing gate-state.json and the circuit-breaker checkpoint as-is; the new field just exposes that same state to machine callers.
4. (Optional hardening) Distinguish completion-path runs from advisory/manual runs so a manual `gate check` pre-check does not inflate the counter backlogit relies on — e.g. a `--advisory-run`/`--no-count` flag or caller tag. Backlogit intends to be the authoritative caller in the completion path.
5. Document the `repeated_failure` field in docs/gates-reference.md under the CLI contract / correction report section.

CONSUMER CONTRACT (how backlogit uses it): reached=true & action=block -> backlogit moves task to queued; reached=true & action=escalate -> backlogit moves task to blocked; below-threshold blocked -> backlogit refuses (task stays active); pass -> backlogit completes. Backlogit will NOT stack a second circuit breaker on this signal (autoharness gate breaker is authoritative).

SOURCE: backlogit gate-broker deliberation + autoharness gate-CLI coordination review, 2026-07-05. Cross-ref backlogit stash D23DFA0B; design doc backlogit repo docs/design-docs/2026-07-04-pre-task-completion-gate-broker.md (section: Deliberation outcomes and autoharness coordination).

Harness unreliably waits for GitHub Copilot reviews to complete before merging in admin mode. Need a more reliable enforcement mechanism to (a) block merge until Copilot review completes when Copilot reviews are enabled, and (b) resolve review comments iteratively across potentially multiple Copilot review rounds (PR rules may trigger more than one round) before merge. Strengthen the admin-merge gate so --admin cannot bypass the wait-for-Copilot-review + comment-resolution loop.

Design a unified, technology-agnostic CI + local-enforcement approach for most repos, and templatize it as an autoharness CI primitive. Synthesize the best of three sibling patterns: (1) docline .github/workflows/ci.yml — every job has an explicit name: attribute (ruff lint, ruff format check, pyright, pytest, sdist+wheel) so it is branch-rule-referenceable; expensive test/build jobs are title-guarded to skip chore:/docs: PRs; and a single always-running ci-gate job (name: ci gate, if: always(), needs all jobs, fails only if a needed job result is failure/cancelled, treats skipped as OK) is the ONE required status check in branch protection — solving the required-check x skipped-job gotcha. (2) backlogit ci.yml — dorny/paths-filter with predicate-quantifier every over a denylist so any non-doc change fails CLOSED into the expensive gate. (3) graphtor ci.yml — regular PR/push CI intentionally stays Linux-only (single OS) because the operator devbox (Windows) already runs local build/test, and macOS/Windows verification is deferred to release.yml on v* tags; a lightweight always-running changes job (name: detect code changes) computes a code=true/false output that gates the heavy build. REQUIREMENTS: every job must carry a name: attribute for branch-rule referencing; the aggregation ci-gate job is the single required check; regular CI is Linux-only to avoid redundant cross-OS runs already covered locally; path/denylist filtering skips docs/backlog/.autoharness-only changes. IMPLICATION (critical): because remote CI is intentionally minimal/Linux-only and trusts local verification, the harness MUST enforce local build+test+lint+format+typecheck BEFORE push to a remote branch/PR, preferably via a git hook mechanism (pre-push and/or pre-commit) that runs the workspace-appropriate quality gates and blocks the push on failure. Deliver both a language-agnostic CI-workflow template (per-ecosystem job internals swappable: uv/ruff/pyright/pytest for Python, cargo fmt/clippy/test/audit for Rust, etc.) and a local pre-push hook enforcement template, wired through workspace discovery so the installed harness picks the right toolchain. Relates to BA28AE56 (autoharness needs its own ci.yml), 0B3F546C (fail-closed denylist CI primitive), and 027B60E8 (deterministic PR-gating hooks catalog) — these four form the unified CI + local-gating group.

Add a CI build/test workflow (ci.yml) for the autoharness Python code and evaluate its validity. The repo main ruleset (18743825 main-protected) requires a status check named build, but NO workflow currently produces it (only release.yml, which triggers on v* tags), so PRs stay pending on build forever and require --admin to bypass. A real PR-triggered CI workflow should run the Python test suite (PYTHONPATH=src python -m unittest discover -s tests, ~325 tests) plus any lint, and report a build check so the required status check is genuinely satisfied without admin bypass. Evaluate whether to adopt the backlogit-style fail-closed denylist path-filter + always-running aggregation gate job as the single required check (see stash 0B3F546C, the CI primitive) so docs-only PRs skip the expensive job while the aggregation gate still reports success. Relates to 0B3F546C (templatize fail-closed CI primitive) and 027B60E8 (deterministic PR-gating mechanisms). Dogfood: whatever pattern is chosen should also be reflected as an autoharness CI-workflow template so target workspaces get the same gating.

Templatize the fail-closed denylist CI pattern (+ aggregation gate) as an autoharness CI primitive. Source: backlogit .github/workflows/ci.yml — a 'changes' job using dorny/paths-filter with predicate-quantifier: 'every' and a denylist ('**' minus !**/*.md, !docs/**, !state-dirs) so any non-docs change fails CLOSED into the expensive lint/vet/test gate (unknown/new file types always run CI). Package as a language-agnostic CI-workflow template/capability-pack primitive: reusable 'changes' detection job + conditional expensive test job (swap per-ecosystem internals) + an always-running aggregation 'CI gate' job (needs:[changes,test], if: always(), asserts success-or-skipped) that is the single REQUIRED status check — solving the required-check x skipped-job gotcha in branch-protected repos. Include SHA-pinned actions, least-privilege permissions, concurrency cancellation. Relates to 027B60E8 (deterministic PR-gating mechanisms) and 0B278CEE (merge-gate reliability).
Identify all hooks and other deterministic mechanisms for gating PRs through layered code reviews and local builds, lint operations, and test harness runs

Backlog archival is leaving the local main worktree diverged because backlogit archival operations (move --status done, archive) perform a HARD DELETE plus physical filesystem move of the .md from .backlogit/queue/ to .backlogit/archive/ rather than a git-tracked move. Git then sees a working-tree deletion of the queue file plus an untracked archive file; because harness hygiene currently leaves .backlogit churn uncommitted, these accumulate across sessions into persistent divergence (the current set of foreign uncommitted changes on local main). FIX: use git mv semantics for backlog archival file relocations so the queue-to-archive move is recorded as a clean, tracked, committable rename. WIRE THIS INTO AGENT OPERATING LOGIC: Stage, Ship, and closure agents must DEFAULT to git mv for backlogit archival operations rather than raw filesystem moves left dangling, and commit the rename as part of the shipment/closure. Two viable mechanisms: (a) perform the relocation with git mv and have backlogit reconcile its own index, or (b) run the backlogit move, then deterministically stage the resulting delete plus untracked add (git add both the old queue path and the new archive path) so git records a rename, then commit. Touch points: .ship.agent.md safe-close (P-015), templates/skills/shipment-reconcile, and the workflow-policy Required Check that inspects git status of archive/ for working-tree deletions (the known backlogit archival quirk that moves files then deletes the archive copies). Must still distinguish archival .md renames (SHOULD be git mv plus committed) from unrelated JSONL log and stash.jsonl churn (not blindly committed). Also plan a one-time reconciliation of the already-accumulated divergence on local main. COORDINATION WITH BACKLOGIT (IMPORTANT): the operator is ALSO fixing this upstream in the backlogit tooling itself, wiring backlogit to use git mv for archival operations rather than physical filesystem operations. So autoharness must COORDINATE with backlogit and NOT double-handle or conflict: version/capability-gate the harness behavior. If the installed backlogit version natively emits git-tracked moves, the harness DEFERS to it and only needs to commit the already-tracked rename (no wrapping); if not, the harness wraps it via mechanism (a) or (b). Detect capability explicitly (backlogit version and/or a config flag), do not assume. The backlog-tool-registry abstraction is the natural coordination point: add a field indicating whether the tool performs git-native archival moves, and drive agent behavior off that flag so autoharness and backlogit stay in sync as backlogit evolves. Track the minimum backlogit version that provides native git-mv archival.

Remove the default --remote option for GitHub Copilot CLI from start.ps1 and make it opt-in via an environment variable (default false).

Current behavior (force-on): start.ps1 (~line 129) and templates/scripts/start.ps1.tmpl (~line 153) always append --remote unless the user already passed it: "if (-not ($args -contains ''--remote'')) { $copilotArguments += ''--remote'' }". This forces remote mode by default.

Desired behavior:
1. Remove the default-on block so --remote is NOT auto-added.
2. Add a new env var (suggested name COPILOT_USE_REMOTE) to templates/scripts/.env.local.tmpl, default false, with a documenting comment. Because .env.local is generated once and never overwritten, also document the knob so existing installs can add it manually.
3. Modify start.ps1 / start.ps1.tmpl to append --remote only when the env var is truthy (true/1) AND the user did not already pass --remote (avoid a duplicate flag).

Scope / parity notes:
- Dogfood parity (repo convention): apply to BOTH the rendered root start.ps1 AND templates/scripts/start.ps1.tmpl.
- Operator specified start.ps1 only; confirm during planning whether start.sh / start.sh.tmpl should get the same knob for shell/PowerShell parity.
- Preserve the .env.local loader semantics (sets var only when unset; strips one matching quote pair).
- Update docs/environment-setup.md if it enumerates .env.local keys.

Add a capability to the auto-mergeinstall and auto-tune agents to maintain each repo GitHub Copilot code-review custom instructions. IMPORTANT CURRENT STATE: repos already have a .github/copilot-instructions.md, but they are outdated/unmaintained and not optimized for GitHub Copilot pull-request reviews. So the primary job is to TUNE these existing files to be current, valid, and useful (generate fresh only when absent; back up before overwriting, never blind-clobber existing operator content). The refreshed instructions must tell Copilot to IGNORE concerns the harness already enforces (formatting/style, markdownlint, workflow policies P-0xx, TDD red-green gates, merge-commit and branch discipline, backlog/shipment mechanics, and anything covered by the adversarial review personas and CLI gates) and to FOCUS on high-value concerns: real logic bugs, security vulnerabilities, secret/credential exposure, injection, incorrect error handling, concurrency/race issues, API misuse, data-loss risks, and high-blast-radius changes. mergeinstall refreshes/optimizes the file (or generates it when absent) from the workspace profile at install time; auto-tune detects drift/staleness and updates it. Canonical path is .github/copilot-instructions.md (repository custom instructions). Stage must add a parameterized template under templates/, wire it into the install-harness and tune-harness skills plus the two agent definitions, and add verification + drift checks. Content must be customized per the discovered harness composition so it references the actual installed policies, gates, and review personas (avoid duplicating what they already catch). SOURCE OF DOCUMENTATION for how to build this custom instructions file (authoritative reference for the canonical path, file format, and precedence rules): https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-on-github/customize-copilot/add-custom-instructions/add-repository-instructions
